trigger: Before authoring a mission package or W0 brief; when 4+ clusters in a CF chain accumulate
summary: Closure-verification gate for multi-cluster CF chains + re-framing review pattern + W1 brief-error catch + scope-expansion mid-mission + fact-check corrections + session-time accumulation ceiling

trigger: Writing or modifying vitest tests; perf-assertion authoring; flake debugging
summary: Audit-method conventions (hookTimeout, ORDER-BY tiebreaker, perf-warmup, skip-guard env-vars) + CF closure-verification templates + flake catalog + meta-test skip-guards against gitignored runtime artifacts + perf-assertion regex coverage + plain-bullet lesson format

trigger: Touching tools/pre-tag-gate.sh, T14 ship steps, or hook scripts
summary: Apply-to-self enforcement, CI-gate override rationale (enumerated CSV per Lesson #10185), no Co-Authored-By trailers (v1.49.621), T14 step ordering (Lesson #10197), dev/main 0-commit drift via ship-sync, cross-link-strict flag, gh-CLI background-task git-discovery quirk

trigger: Editing .claude/{skills,agents,hooks}/ paths; staging files into .planning/, .claude/, .archive/, artifacts/, .gsd/, .chipset/, .env, project-claude/hooks/.log/
summary: PreToolUse hook BLOCKS self-mod operations without SC_SELF_MOD=1 / SC_INSTALL_CALLER=project-claude / npm_lifecycle_event override. git-add-blocker.js BLOCKS protected-path staging without SC_FORCE_ADD=1. settings.json gsd-config-guard-protected; new hooks route via project-claude/. Mission packages never committed.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.585 C01 + C02; extended v1.49.653; lessons appended v1.49.654

trigger: Touching src-tauri/src/security/keystore.rs or wrapping Node CLI surfaces; Argon2id + age v0.10 invariants
summary: OS keyring (preferred) → age-encrypted file (fallback). Legacy plaintext keystore retired v1.49.637.

trigger: Authoring W1 substrate-axis declarations; relocating substrate-probe docs into tracked docs/ from .planning/
summary: Substrate-form declaration patterns + LOCKED/SUSTAINED state-machine + forward-shadow first-instance convention + 4-LEVEL trophic cascade closure + aquatic-substrate-pivot + auction-theory winner's-curse pattern + HARD-BLOCK forbidden-substring detection at W2 verification

trigger: Authoring retrospective V-flag emit/close/state blocks; running T14 step 2.6 (citation-debt auto-update)
summary: Formal '### V-flag emit/close/state:' markdown blocks → scan-retrospectives.mjs --write-diff → apply-diff.mjs. Closes V-flag drift between retrospectives and the ledger.

trigger: T14 ship sequence step 2.5; observing pre-tag-gate step 12 WARN about STORY.md drift
summary: Public docs/release-notes/STORY.md auto-append gate (T14 step 2.5) closes Lesson #10197 silent-no-op via per-tag append + idempotent re-run. Pre-tag-gate step 12 surfaces accumulated drift.

trigger: Authoring W2/W3 sub-agent dispatch prompts; spawning parallel build agents; sizing token budgets per dispatch; embedding chapter-generation in NASA build dispatches
summary: Sub-agents lack SendMessage in toolkit (spawn-task-return-result, not continuous peers); ~60-70 tool-use ceiling per dispatch; bound dispatches to ~1 component / 30-50min; commit-between-deliverables discipline; chunked Write+Edit append pattern for >32K HTML deliverables; transient API errors recover via identical-prompt retry; 64K output-token cap requires JSON+HTML split; W3.5 chapter-gen via `run-with-pg.mjs refresh --fast --quiet` belongs in the dispatch deliverable list (v709 codification)
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.654 (new domain — codified from v1.49.637-651 lesson cluster); v1.49.709 (W3.5 chapter-gen bake-in)

trigger: Planning a non-degree-advancing milestone; converting accumulated social-rule debt into deterministic gates; meta-testing newly-introduced gates at ship time
summary: Counter-cadence cleanup-mission pattern productive every ~30 forward milestones; gate-not-vigilance discipline (convert offending rule to a gate, not a re-emphasized prose rule); meta-test newly-installed gates against the milestone's own release-notes during T14; discipline-as-code in 3-cluster lifecycle; cluster-close forward-notes are load-bearing decisions; phantom-deps npm-audit fix + probe threshold gap; finer-grained ~5-1-1 alternation as the steady-state maintenance complement to 30-ship batched cleanup (#10430)
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.654 (new domain — codified from v1.49.585-651 lesson cluster); finer-cadence pattern appended v1.49.805 (S7 lever)

trigger: Before executing any REVIEW ledger entry or planning artifact written as a sweep summary; when authoring audit rules and audit verdicts
summary: Per-file recon (~15min) precedes per-file code (verify scope, framing, prior-art). Audit rules classify by BEHAVIOR (e.g. `node:fs` import presence), not by filename pattern. Interface-conformance audits report a 3-way verdict: ALREADY CONFORMS / SHOULD CONFORM / INTENTIONALLY DIFFERENT (third verdict is load-bearing — prevents future re-flagging). REVIEW ledger fields distinguish filename-collisions (rename-scale) from class-name-collisions (merge-scale).
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.784 (new domain — codified from v1.49.780-782 lesson cluster)

trigger: Introducing a chokepoint, interface, or generator-template to N existing modules with high call-site multiplicity; authoring a generator that may run on partial input; adding the SECOND instance of a class-typed family
summary: Optional `ctx?` parameter pattern for chokepoint retrofit — three states (undefined→legacy permissive; defaultLoaderContext()→audit-only; restricted→enforced); zero call-site churn. Tolerant generators emit skip-the-line over `UNKNOWN`/`TODO`/placeholder sentinels — placeholders pollute output, break round-trip, and mask the schema gap. Exception: emit the default when the field has a load-bearing default value. Cross-class registries extract at the SECOND class instance (not first, not third) — the contrast between two real instances determines the registry shape; deferring to the third class ships a temporary wrong-source measure that has to be unwound.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.784 (new domain — codified from v1.49.782 LoaderContext + v1.49.783 normalizer fix lesson cluster); v1.49.802 (extended with #10426 second-instance cross-class registry extraction from T1.1 arc)

trigger: When a handoff lists a fix as 'escalated' or 'load-bearing'; when a test in `tests/integration/` has been red across 2+ consecutive ships; when operator memory accumulates workarounds for a known broken tool
summary: Close escalated wedges within 1-2 milestones; do not defer beyond that. The cost of deferral is silent test drift + workaround documentation accumulation, not the eventual fix. Forward-cadence engine-state work can wait one milestone; an open red test in `tests/integration/` cannot. The pre-tag-gate's silent acceptance of a known-failing test is the alarm.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.784 (new domain — codified from v1.49.783 STATE.md normalizer fix)

trigger: Authoring or extending a CLI tool in `tools/` that scans the codebase, emits metrics, or produces a comparable report; designing its test harness; piping its output to another command
summary: Test harnesses use spawnSync (not execSync) so stderr survives exit 0; cross-module analyzers scan multiple importer roots (not just src/); metric-emitting tools commit a baseline file so future runs diff via git; CLIs flush stdout before process.exit() to avoid 64KB pipe-buffer truncation; diff-emitting observability tools document the warm-up period (first run has no prior baseline); version-stamped baseline writers refuse to overwrite existing files with different content unless --force (v794 closure of v791 trip). #10421 field-validated at v789 by adoption-baseline diff emitting exactly the predicted line.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.790 (new domain — codified from v1.49.785-789 adoption-telemetry build chain lesson cluster); v1.49.794 (extended with #10424 refuse-to-overwrite guard)

trigger: Authoring a per-module shelfware verdict (WIRED / RETIRED / ALLOWLISTED) for a module flagged by the adoption-scan as test-only; selecting the wire site that satisfies a WIRED verdict
summary: Observability surface (scanner + dashboard + allowlist) and decision surface (per-module verdicts) MUST live in separate files and evolve independently. Default to the lightest wire that flips the adoption-scan classification — a CLI subcommand usually suffices and preserves HARD-preservation invariants — defer richer in-loop wires to follow-on ships only if the verdict explicitly requires production use.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.790 (new domain — codified from v1.49.789 first shelfware verdict lesson cluster)

trigger: Designing or extending the bounded-learning calibration loop in `src/bounded-learning/`; choosing the statistical primitive for an anytime-valid threshold-update decision; adding a new calibratable threshold class
summary: Two-sided likelihood-ratio e-processes on bounded binary observations at |x|=1 are insensitive to unanimous direction (cosh(λ)·exp(−λ²/2) ≤ 1 for all λ ≥ 0); use two one-sided e-processes at Bonferroni α/2 instead. Math-check-before-test-fixture convention: compute E_n/E_{n-1} under unanimous evidence by hand before writing the trip-point assertion — if the ratio is ≤ 1 the construction is unfit. Per-class observation sources are necessary surface; the lightest-wire discipline does NOT apply to necessary surface (cross-ref Architecture-retrofit patterns #10426).
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.802 (new domain — codified from v1.49.795 T1.1 ship 1 e-process design lesson)

trigger: Designing a new surface (function, module, CLI flag, skill prompt section, hook) where silent-vs-loud failure asymmetry is in play; adding a `try { ... } catch { /* ignore */ }` block; reviewing a long-running primitive's transient-error handling
summary: Forensic, dashboard, and observability surfaces fail silently; load-bearing surfaces fail loudly. Test: does the user's next decision depend on this surface's output? If yes → load-bearing → propagate. If no → accessory → swallow at surface boundary. Contract documentation convention: explicit `// best-effort silent` or `// fail loudly` marker in docstring paired with at least one test assertion that exercises the failure path; surfaces without the paired test are stochastic.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.802 (new domain — codified from v1.49.799-801 three-instance audit-log + watch-loop + /sc:status lesson cluster)

trigger: Planning a counter-cadence cleanup-mission; deciding which operational debt to spend a non-degree-advancing ship on; auditing whether the project's three operational axes (codify / consume / calibrate) are in balance
summary: Three operational axes — codify (~7-10 ships), consume (≤6 ships from substrate to first non-test caller), calibrate (~10 ships per wired threshold tick). Cadence-overdue check is prose-only and informational: each axis names its overdue trigger; the operator picks; the next counter-cadence's scope discussion NAMES the proposed axis even if a different axis wins on operator priority. The naming is the load-bearing discipline; the choice is operator-bounded.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.805 (new domain — codified from S3 lever in 2026-05-26 core-functions audit retrospective)

trigger: Authoring docs that surface opt-in flags to operators; reviewing whether a new substrate ship needs a public-surface entry; deciding what a new operator's second-30-minutes path should look like
summary: Per-substrate four-field shape: what it unlocks / what it costs / opt-in mechanic / when to defer. Public surface (this doc) is the WHY-truth source; `docs/MODULE-DEFAULTS.md` is the flag-truth source. Add new entries below their axis grouping (observability / learning / execution / safety) in ship order. Retire entries only on codebase removal, not on default-flip (note inline). Cross-link to the canonical flag-truth doc; do NOT duplicate flag tables.
codified_at_milestone: v1.49.805 (new domain — codified from S4 lever in 2026-05-26 core-functions audit retrospective)
